frameworks.name: multistore model of memory
frameworks.one_line: Atkinson-Shiffrin separation of sensory register, short-term and long-term stores.

frameworks.name: multicomponent working memory model
frameworks.one_line: Baddeley-Hitch visuospatial and phonological stores coordinated by a central executive.

frameworks.name: scope and control of attention model
frameworks.one_line: Cowan's attentional activation and maintenance account of working memory.

frameworks.name: individual differences model
frameworks.one_line: Engle's interplay of primary memory, attentional control, and secondary memory.

core_question: How have theoretical models of working memory evolved and how are they applied to education?
key_assumptions: working memory is a capacity-limited system for immediate information processing
memory can be decomposed into functionally distinct stores or components
attentional control modulates maintenance of active information
key_findings: Miller's seven-plus-or-minus-two capacity limit framed the construct
successive models partition working memory into stores plus executive or attentional control
Cowan and Engle frameworks foreground attention rather than dedicated stores
interpretations: working memory is best understood as a family of complementary models rather than one architecture
model-based measures can guide individualized instructional strategies
out_of_scope_note: Encyclopedia review article surveying historical models of working memory; no new empirical manipulations or findings.
